name: Choking
summary: Object blocking airway. Person can't speak, cough or breathe normally.

callEmergency.text: Call 999 if back blows + abdominal/chest thrusts don't clear it, OR if the person becomes unconscious.

steps: Encourage them to cough hard — this clears it in most cases.
If not clearing: stand to the side, slightly behind. Support chest with one hand. Lean them well forwards.
5 sharp back blows between the shoulder blades with the heel of your other hand.
If still choking: 5 abdominal thrusts. Stand behind. Arms round upper abdomen. Lean them forward.
Clench fist between navel and ribcage. Grasp with the other hand. Pull sharply INWARDS and UPWARDS.
Alternate 5 back blows with 5 abdominal thrusts.
If still not cleared — call 999. Keep alternating until help arrives or they become unconscious.
Support infant face-down across your lap, head supported, lower than chest.
Don't compress the soft tissues under the jaw.
5 sharp back blows with heel of hand between the shoulder blades.
Aim to dislodge with each blow, not give all 5.
Turn infant face-up, head still lower than chest.
5 CHEST thrusts — similar to CPR compressions but sharper and slower.
Alternate 5 back blows with 5 chest thrusts.
Call 999. Continue until help arrives or infant becomes unconscious.

warnings.text: NEVER perform abdominal thrusts on an infant under 1 year. Use chest thrusts.
Don't put fingers into the mouth to try to dig the object out.
